sql_array: CREATE TABLE json_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data JSON);
SELECT json.keyarray(data) AS keys, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM json_test) * 2 AS count_doubled FROM json_test WHERE data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT json.keyarray(data) FROM json_test WHERE id = 1) * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM json_test WHERE data IS NOT NULL) AS computed_value;
CREATE TABLE another_test (key_id INT PRIMARY KEY, key_value JSON NOT NULL);

SELECT a.key_id, a.key_value, (json.keyarray(a.key_value) || json.keyarray(b.data)) * a.key_id AS complex_calc FROM another_test a JOIN json_test b ON a.key_id = b.id WHERE json.keyarray(b.data) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (json.keyarray(data) * 10) AS multiplied_keys_count FROM json_test HAVING multiplied_keys_count > 5;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE complex_test AS SELECT id, json.keyarray(data) AS keys_array FROM json_test;
SELECT keys_array * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM complex_test WHERE keys_array IS NOT NULL) AS final_result FROM complex_test WHERE keys_array IS NOT NULL;
